name: Don Sickler
abstract: Don Sickler (born January 6, 1944) is an American jazz trumpeter, arranger, and producer. In the 1980s, he set up a tribute band called Dameronia, with Philly Joe Jones, to play the music of Tadd Dameron.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Charlie Haden's Liberation Music Orchestra/Philly Joe Jones' Dameronia

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: So now there’s a couple of guys, friends of mine now, they’re let me see, Dean Pratt and Don Sickler.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Rudy Van Gelder
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript only contains a note mentioning 'Don and Maureen Sickler' in the context of the recording session description. The interviewee (Rudy Van Gelder) does not speak about Don Sickler, nor does the interviewer ask about a personal relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rudy Van Gelder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: So we met with Don Sickler, who was handling Monk’s publishing company, found out which tunes were in his company.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: In fact they did a project, the live recording we did in Montreux. He was doing the editing and all that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: Don Sickler would always tell me, “Damn, you and Thad Jones…”
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
